rawMemo:
Working title: The Clockmaker Under Glass.

Mira Vale repairs antique clocks in a floodlit arcade that closes every night at 9:17. Her missing brother left a brass moth in the workshop drawer and a note that says the city council has been selling minutes from abandoned lives. The old observatory above North Bell Station still rings at noon even though its bell was removed during the winter curfew.

People: Mira Vale, Toma Vale, Councillor Iven Rusk, the archivist known as Sister Hal.
Places: North Bell Station, the glass arcade, the old observatory, the canal market.
Objects: brass moth, ledger of minutes, cracked station clock, black ribbon key.

The story should feel intimate and strange, but not explain the magic too early. Need to decide whether Toma is alive, whether the council is villainous or desperate, and whether the brass moth is a spy, a key, or a memory container.

Possible ending: Mira refuses to restore the city's stolen time and instead gives one lost hour back to every person who was erased from the records.

profiles.title: Mira Vale
profiles.summary: Clock repairer with personal stakes in the stolen-time mystery.
open_questions: How much does Mira know before the first scene?
What does repair work cost her emotionally?

profiles.title: Toma Vale
profiles.summary: Missing brother whose fate controls the story's grief, hope, and final reveal.

profiles.title: City council
profiles.summary: Institutional actor connected to selling or reallocating stolen time.

profiles.title: Brass moth
profiles.summary: Compact story engine that may serve as spy, key, or memory container.
open_questions: Can it be explained visually without exposition?

claims.title: Mira Vale repairs antique clocks in the glass arcade.
claims.claimText: Mira Vale repairs antique clocks in the glass arcade.
claims.notes: Safe adoption candidate because it names the protagonist role without deciding the magic system.

claims.title: The glass arcade closes every night at 9:17.
claims.claimText: The glass arcade closes every night at 9:17.
claims.notes: Useful recurring cue; adoption does not decide why the time matters.

claims.title: The city council sells minutes from abandoned lives.
claims.claimText: The city council sells minutes from abandoned lives.
claims.notes: High-value premise claim, but adoption should not decide whether the council is villainous, desperate, divided, or misled.

claims.title: The council may be desperate rather than villainous.
claims.claimText: The council may be desperate rather than villainous.
claims.notes: A motive route, not canon. Keep it reversible until the human author chooses Council motive.

claims.title: Toma Vale left a note about the stolen minutes.
claims.claimText: Toma Vale left a note about the stolen minutes.
claims.notes: The note can exist without deciding whether Toma is alive, dead, erased, transformed, or deliberately unknown.

claims.title: The brass moth is a spy, a key, or a memory container.
claims.claimText: The brass moth is a spy, a key, or a memory container.
claims.notes: Do not adopt a final function in this ledger; choose only after the first-use rule is approved.

claims.title: The observatory rings at noon even though its bell was removed.
claims.claimText: The observatory rings at noon even though its bell was removed.
claims.notes: Keep as an intentional impossible image unless later continuity review rejects it.

claims.title: A winter curfew happened before the present action.
claims.claimText: A winter curfew happened before the present action.
claims.notes: In-world history only; do not treat as sourced real-world history.

claims.title: Mira gives one lost hour back to every person erased from the records.
claims.claimText: Mira gives one lost hour back to every person erased from the records.
claims.notes: Ending route only. Keep held or provisional until Toma fate and Council motive are chosen.

unresolvedCreativeDecisions.title: Toma fate
unresolvedCreativeDecisions.viewer_facing_information: Toma is missing and emotionally central.
unresolvedCreativeDecisions.production_only_note: Do not script a final reveal until the author chooses alive, dead, erased, transformed, or deliberately unknown.
unresolvedCreativeDecisions.next_move: Choose a provisional fate before adopting the ending or series arc.

unresolvedCreativeDecisions.title: Brass moth function
unresolvedCreativeDecisions.viewer_facing_information: The brass moth is a charged clue.
unresolvedCreativeDecisions.production_only_note: Keep spy, key, and memory-container options reversible until the first-use rule is chosen.
unresolvedCreativeDecisions.next_move: Adopt one first-act function or leave the object held.

unresolvedCreativeDecisions.title: Council motive
unresolvedCreativeDecisions.viewer_facing_information: The council is tied to stolen time.
unresolvedCreativeDecisions.production_only_note: Do not frame the council as final villain or tragic caretaker until the author decides.
unresolvedCreativeDecisions.next_move: Mark villainous, desperate, divided, misled, or hold.

taskCards.title: Choose Toma's minimum fate state
taskCards.recommended_timing: Before adopting the ending or series arc.
taskCards.creative_utility: Controls grief, hope, and mystery payoff.
taskCards.risk_if_overdecided_now: Mystery tension may collapse before the premise is tested.
taskCards.minimum_decision: Pick alive, dead, erased, transformed, or deliberately unknown.
taskCards.provisional_option: Keep Toma missing but decide what evidence Mira finds first.
taskCards.residual: Purpose: stabilize stakes. Effect: constrains ending. Requirements: author choice. State: unresolved. Owner: human author. Next move: select provisional fate.

taskCards.title: Set brass moth function for first act only
taskCards.recommended_timing: Before drafting the first three scenes.
taskCards.creative_utility: Gives the protagonist a concrete investigative action.
taskCards.risk_if_ignored: The object stays atmospheric but inert.
taskCards.risk_if_overdecided_now: A full magic system may overexplain the strongest mystery.
taskCards.minimum_decision: Decide what the moth does once on page or screen.
taskCards.provisional_option: It opens one sealed drawer and stores one audible memory.
taskCards.residual: Purpose: make clue usable. Effect: enables scene action. Requirements: first-use rule. State: candidate. Owner: human author. Next move: adopt or revise first-use rule.

taskCards.title: Keep all visual assets as placeholders
taskCards.recommended_timing: Before any public-facing storyboard or video assembly.
taskCards.creative_utility: Allows outline review without implying release-ready media.
taskCards.risk_if_ignored: Placeholder images can be mistaken for approved assets.
taskCards.risk_if_overdecided_now: Asset sourcing can distract from story structure.
taskCards.minimum_decision: Label all visuals as placeholder or not needed.
taskCards.provisional_option: Use text-only shot descriptions for this MVP.
taskCards.residual: Purpose: prevent rights drift. Effect: blocks accidental release assumptions. Requirements: rights review later. State: local-only. Owner: producer. Next move: keep placeholder labels visible.

qaGateResults.finding: Nucleus inferred for The Clockmaker Under Glass: clockmaker versus stolen time, but Toma's fate is unresolved.
qaGateResults.next_move: Adopt a one-sentence nucleus or keep it provisional.

qaGateResults.finding: Council motive, Toma fate, and brass moth function are intentionally undecided.
qaGateResults.next_move: Keep unresolved choices in task cards before adopting canon.

qaGateResults.finding: All claims in this MVP point back to the raw memo or derived candidate status.
qaGateResults.next_move: Add external sources only after a source review workflow exists.

qaGateResults.finding: Story order, historical context, and production order are labeled separately.
qaGateResults.next_move: Preserve sequence_scope on future timeline records.

scenes.label: Hook
scenes.beat: The bell rings over an empty tower.
scenes.visual_intent: Show impossible image; keep source as memo-only.

text_cues.text: At noon, the bell rings from a tower with no bell.

scenes.label: Premise
scenes.beat: Mira finds the brass moth and Toma's note.
scenes.visual_intent: Use close detail and one prop action.

text_cues.text: Someone has been selling minutes from abandoned lives.

scenes.label: Decision

text_cues.text: What would you give back to the erased?

generatedOutlines.title: 3-minute video
generatedOutlines.purpose: Review short narrative shape with a midpoint turn.

scenes.label: Setup
scenes.visual_intent: Lean on sound and recurring time cue.

text_cues.text: Every night, the arcade closes at 9:17.

scenes.label: Investigation
scenes.beat: The moth opens one record tied to North Bell Station.

text_cues.text: The first stolen minute belongs to someone no one remembers.
The council may be saving the city by erasing its poorest ghosts.

scenes.label: Choice
scenes.visual_intent: Point to ending candidate without locking it.

text_cues.text: A city can keep time, or it can keep faith.

generatedOutlines.title: 10-minute video
generatedOutlines.purpose: Review full explainer-style outline before any automated generation.

scenes.label: Cold open
scenes.beat: Bell paradox and workshop clock rhythm.

text_cues.text: The noon bell rings from an empty frame.

scenes.label: World rules
scenes.beat: Minutes can be harvested from abandoned lives.
scenes.visual_intent: Present as rumor and ledger evidence, not exposition dump.

text_cues.text: Time is currency only when nobody remembers the owner.

scenes.label: Character pressure
scenes.beat: Mira must choose Toma or the erased citizens.

text_cues.text: One hour can save a brother or restore a city of names.

scenes.label: Resolution candidate
scenes.beat: Lost hours return to erased people.

text_cues.text: The clocks do not restart. The people do.

generatedOutlines.title: series candidate
generatedOutlines.purpose: Explore whether the premise supports multiple episodes.

scenes.label: Episode engine
scenes.beat: Each episode restores or investigates one stolen life.

text_cues.text: Every recovered hour reveals a different crime.

scenes.label: Season pressure
scenes.visual_intent: Delay final motive until season midpoint.

text_cues.text: The enemy is not the council. It is the bargain they inherited.

scenes.label: Final question
scenes.beat: What does the city owe people it erased?

text_cues.text: A restored hour is not the same as justice.
